title: Grift Of The Magi

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams] - Get me down!
jokes.setup: Homer gets into some physical predicament during opening credits
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer 'D'oh!' followed by screaming for help
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer catchphrase moment - high character integration but predictable

jokes.text: The ozone hole that devastated Brazil last summer is apparently wintering in Springfield. Whoo! Springfield rocks!
jokes.setup: News report about dangerous ozone hole affecting Springfield
jokes.punchline: Newscaster's enthusiastic 'Springfield rocks!' about environmental disaster
jokes.explanation: Sharp juxtaposition of disaster news with cheerful local pride - well-crafted dark humor

jokes.text: Experts recommend a class nine or Robin Williams level of hair coverage
jokes.setup: Technical classification system for sun protection
jokes.punchline: Robin Williams as the extreme example of hairiness
jokes.explanation: Clever celebrity reference that creates vivid mental image while serving the plot

jokes.text: Aw, the zoning disk is warped.
jokes.setup: Kids looking for something fun to play with
jokes.punchline: Disappointment over a boring board game component
jokes.explanation: Subtle absurdity in the mundane nature of their entertainment options

jokes.text: Look. You're Milhouse. Who wets their bed now, Milhouse?
jokes.setup: Bart spraying Silly String on Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Bart mocking Milhouse about bedwetting while covering him in string
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate cruel humor from Bart, though fairly standard

jokes.text: You want to dress up like ladies? Uh, wouldn't that make us kind of fruity?
jokes.setup: Boys looking for something to do in parents' closet
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's innocent concern about cross-dressing
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate humor about gender roles and childhood innocence

jokes.text: What's the matter? Scared you might like it?
jokes.setup: Milhouse expressing reluctance about dressing up
jokes.punchline: Bart's psychological manipulation through challenging masculinity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's manipulative nature in kid-appropriate way

jokes.text: Oh, wow. This really hides my thighs. [Purrs]
jokes.setup: Boys trying on women's clothing
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's unexpectedly sophisticated commentary and purring
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - nerdy Milhouse showing unexpected vanity and sensuality

jokes.text: Sisters are doing it for themselves
jokes.setup: Boys dressed as women feeling empowered
jokes.punchline: Singing feminist anthem while in drag
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag with cultural reference that fits the moment

jokes.text: What's going on? And I want a non gay explanation!
jokes.setup: Homer finding boys in women's clothing
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt demand for heterosexual explanation
jokes.explanation: Very Homer response - direct and inappropriate but character-consistent

jokes.text: Uh, we're drunk. Really drunk. Oh, thank God!
jokes.setup: Need for acceptable explanation for cross-dressing
jokes.punchline: Homer's relief that underage drinking is the 'better' explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of parental priorities - Homer preferring alcohol to gender nonconformity

jokes.text: Now, I'm afraid your son has cracked his coccyx. [All Laughing] Sorry.
jokes.setup: Doctor delivering serious medical diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Everyone laughing at the word 'coccyx' and doctor's apology
jokes.explanation: Basic juvenile humor but the doctor's awareness makes it work

jokes.text: Now, son, this is a teaching hospital which is why I equipped the seat of your cast with a viewing window.
jokes.setup: Explaining medical procedures at teaching hospital
jokes.punchline: Cast designed to display Bart's injured bottom for educational purposes
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical solution that creates ongoing visual gag potential

jokes.text: Oh, my. Oh, now that's something. He should exercise more.
jokes.setup: Students observing through the viewing window
jokes.punchline: Clinical commentary on Bart's exposed anatomy
jokes.explanation: Follow-through on the viewing window gag with medical student reactions

jokes.text: Technically, yes, but the building costs would be astronomical.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about required disability access
jokes.punchline: Skinner's bureaucratic excuse for non-compliance
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner character moment - bureaucratic and cost-conscious

jokes.text: Did I hear the word 'astronomical'? If so, my construction outfit, Valdazo Brothers Olive Oil, is poised to help.
jokes.setup: Mention of astronomical building costs
jokes.punchline: Mob construction company with obvious food service front appearing instantly
jokes.explanation: Great character entrance with absurd business name that hints at mob front

jokes.text: In order to avoid certain legal complications, the trucks are always rolling.
jokes.setup: Question about how construction trucks arrived so quickly
jokes.punchline: Explanation suggesting constant criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about mob operations disguised as helpful explanation

jokes.text: Good Lord. Do we really need all those ramps? Who's to say? Does a peacock need all those feathers?
jokes.setup: Skinner questioning excessive ramp construction
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's philosophical non-answer about peacocks
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd deflection that sounds profound but means nothing

jokes.text: Look. You're getting a little philosophical for me. I suppose so. They say it happens in the autumn years.
jokes.setup: Skinner noting Fat Tony's philosophical bent
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's poetic reflection on aging wisdom
jokes.explanation: Nice character development showing Fat Tony's unexpectedly reflective side

jokes.text: When people ask if we're in compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1975, I can say, 'We are closer than ever before!'
jokes.setup: Celebrating new ramp construction
jokes.punchline: Skinner's bureaucratic non-answer that admits continued non-compliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner bureaucratic speak that sounds positive while admitting failure

jokes.text: Don't need it anymore. Doctor says my butt bone's stronger than ever. Ta-da!
jokes.setup: Expected to see Bart in wheelchair for ramp inauguration
jokes.punchline: Bart's recovered and breaks his cast dramatically
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment that undermines the whole ramp necessity

jokes.text: Well, at least we're prepared for the new millennium.
jokes.setup: Expensive ramps built for one temporarily disabled student
jokes.punchline: Skinner's attempt to find silver lining in wasteful spending
jokes.explanation: Good recovery line that shows Skinner trying to justify the absurd expense

jokes.text: My God! The whole thing's made of breadsticks! And paint and shellac. It's all itemized in this bill.
jokes.setup: Discovering the construction details
jokes.punchline: Ramps built from food items and paint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist detail about mob construction methods using food products

jokes.text: $200,000? Are you mad? I don't get mad. I get stabby.
jokes.setup: Skinner shocked by the enormous bill
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's threatening wordplay about anger vs violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent mob threat disguised as wordplay - perfectly menacing and quotable

jokes.text: [All] Yea! Oh, you're cheering now. But someday you'll- Yea! I'm just gonna stop trying. Yea!
jokes.setup: Students cheering school closure
jokes.punchline: Skinner repeatedly interrupted by continued cheering, giving up mid-speech
jokes.explanation: Great escalating frustration gag with Skinner's defeated surrender

jokes.text: Where are the refreshments? You keep asking me that, and I keep telling you over there.
jokes.setup: Town meeting about school crisis
jokes.punchline: Someone obsessed with snacks during emergency meeting
jokes.explanation: Minor background gag showing Springfield citizens' misplaced priorities

jokes.text: I've got a motor home I never use. Maybe we should raffle it off. Maybe you should shut up.
jokes.setup: Citizen offering helpful suggestion
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile shutdown of reasonable idea
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective town meeting dysfunction humor

jokes.text: We could try selling liquor. Uh, I'm doing great. Please, sir. Put some shoes on. What? You don't like my bags?
jokes.setup: Town meeting fundraising suggestions
jokes.punchline: Barney's inappropriate suggestion while apparently underdressed
jokes.explanation: Good Barney character moment with visual component about his disheveled state

jokes.text: He releases the hounds on every charity that comes to his door. Feed the Children. Save the Whales. Even Release the Hounds.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Mr. Burns won't help with charity
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about charity to release hounds getting hounds released on them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about self-referential charity and Burns's consistent cruelty

jokes.text: Too bad I'm an idiot 'cause my school closed. Oh, well. No! That's the rat poison! And freeze.
jokes.setup: School play to convince Burns to donate
jokes.punchline: Deliberately bad acting about poisoning and theatrical direction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing the transparent manipulation of their school play

jokes.text: I can't take Mr. Burns to the 'hospital' 'cause I'm too dumb to read a map.
jokes.setup: Continuing the obvious school play
jokes.punchline: Ralph's air quotes and self-aware stupidity admission
jokes.explanation: Good Ralph moment combining his innocence with theatrical self-awareness

jokes.text: Hello. I'm Dr. Stupid. I'm going to take out your liver bones. Oops. You're dead.
jokes.setup: School play medical scene
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous doctor name, fake anatomy, and immediate patient death
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor with kid-appropriate medical incompetence

jokes.text: Hmm. Limón. Fuerte. Limón. Fuerte. Limón- ¡Ay, ay, ay, ay, ay! ¡Mi estómago!
jokes.setup: Homer watching Spanish TV show about car sales
jokes.punchline: Homer trying to understand Spanish words before getting stomach pain
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about Homer's confusion with foreign language and coincidental pain

jokes.text: Daytime TV is muy estúpido.
jokes.setup: Homer watching and struggling with Spanish television
jokes.punchline: Homer criticizing TV in broken Spanish while being glued to it
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment with Homer's hypocritical TV criticism

jokes.text: Valencia? These are juice oranges.
jokes.setup: Teachers receiving severance packages
jokes.punchline: Skinner's prissy complaint about orange quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment showing his petty focus on details while losing his job

jokes.text: What are you passionate about, partner? Boogers. Boogers! That was great. You know, humor is a sign of intelligence.
jokes.setup: New teacher asking about student passions
jokes.punchline: Teacher enthusiastically validating gross-out humor as intelligence
jokes.explanation: Solid subversion of educational psychology into juvenile validation

jokes.text: Hey, I'm here to make sure that you get a kick out of education. Hi-yah!
jokes.setup: Teacher explaining educational philosophy
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'kick' with karate move
jokes.explanation: Basic pun humor with physical comedy element

jokes.text: Check it out. I'm breaking books at a sixth grade level.
jokes.setup: Bart destroying educational materials
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'reading at grade level' applied to destruction
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay subverting educational assessment terminology

jokes.text: You know what our homework is? Find a toy and bring it to class. Boy, that sounds fun. I know. But I'm still not gonna do it.
jokes.setup: Easy homework assignment about toys
jokes.punchline: Bart's commitment to not doing homework regardless of how easy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment showing his principled opposition to all homework

jokes.text: My busy box. It's got everything. Vroom, vroom, vroom! I'm calling Daddy. Good for you, not being bound by the recommended age.
jokes.setup: Students sharing their toys
jokes.punchline: Milhouse with clearly inappropriate baby toy and teacher's awkward validation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Milhouse's social awkwardness and teacher's forced positivity

jokes.text: Its eyes should be telescopes. No. Periscopes. No. Microscopes! Can you come back to me?
jokes.setup: Describing ideal toy features
jokes.punchline: Rapid changes between similar scientific instruments, asking for timeout
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation humor showing kid's indecisiveness about technical features

jokes.text: Now that's market research you can take to the bank- the money bank.
jokes.setup: Teacher excited about student toy ideas
jokes.punchline: Redundant clarification about what kind of bank
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about corporate speak and unnecessary clarification

jokes.text: Fun? Ralph, there are no right or wrong answers but if you don't pipe down, I'm giving you an 'F.'
jokes.setup: Teacher claiming no wrong answers in brainstorming
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatening bad grade for participation
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction between progressive education theory and authoritarian practice

jokes.text: Ha! The 'ironing' is delicious. The word is 'irony.' Huh?
jokes.setup: Bart attempting to use sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Malapropism followed by confusion when corrected
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart malapropism showing his attempt at intelligence backfiring

jokes.text: Alls I know is I'm gettin' straight A's, and that ain't not bad.
jokes.setup: Bart defending the new educational system
jokes.punchline: Double negative while celebrating academic achievement
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor showing Bart's poor grammar while celebrating grades

jokes.text: I see you. Give me a hug.
jokes.setup: Lisa discovering surveillance equipment
jokes.punchline: Menacing doll with overly affectionate greeting
jokes.explanation: Effective creepy toy humor with contrast between surveillance and affection

jokes.text: Daddy, I'm ready to get out now. Over.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum called away during Ralph's bath time
jokes.punchline: Ralph using radio protocol from bathtub
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ralph moment combining his innocence with police family background

jokes.text: Is this gonna be like one of those horror movies where we open the door and everything's normal and we think you're crazy, but then there really is a killer robot and the next morning you find me impaled on a weather vane? Is that what this is, Lisa?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Chief Wiggum to investigate surveillance room
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's oddly specific and gruesome horror movie knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor with Wiggum's suspiciously detailed horror movie plot knowledge

jokes.text: To be fair, not all evil robots are killers.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum's concern about killer robots
jokes.punchline: Lisa's measured defense of evil robot diversity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa line showing her logical precision applied to absurd situation

jokes.text: Yeah, right, mop top. And I'm Ed Sullivan. 'Really big show.' No, no. I can do it better. 'Really big show. Really big.' That's it.
jokes.setup: Wiggum dismissing Lisa's claims
jokes.punchline: Wiggum practicing Ed Sullivan impression after denying resemblance
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum character moment showing his distractibility and vanity

jokes.text: So have a merry Christmas, happy Hanukkah, 'kwazy' Kwanzaa, a tip-top Tet, and a solemn, dignified Ramadan.
jokes.setup: Krusty giving holiday wishes on TV
jokes.punchline: Progressively awkward attempts at cultural sensitivity with alliteration
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of performative inclusivity with escalating awkwardness

jokes.text: If you don't have Funzo, you're nothin'.
jokes.setup: Commercial for Funzo toy
jokes.punchline: Extremely harsh judgment about children's worth based on toy ownership
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of manipulative toy marketing targeting children's insecurities

jokes.text: But if you're gonna throw a spaz, I'll come with.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Bart to help expose corporate conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual agreement to join her 'spaz' reaction
jokes.explanation: Shows sibling dynamic and Bart's casual approach to Lisa's intensity.

jokes.text: But the menu said 'galaxy of prawns.' Three prawns are hardly a galaxy. What do you mean your hands are tied? Let me talk to Mr. Quan.
jokes.setup: Gary Coleman as security guard having phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Serious complaint about shrimp portion size with astronomical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Gary Coleman's disproportionate food complaint

jokes.text: Yes, Mr. President. I can be in Washington right away.
jokes.setup: Gary Coleman continuing phone conversations while on security duty
jokes.punchline: Claiming to receive call from President while working low-level job
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor about Coleman's delusions of importance

jokes.text: What you talkin' about, Miss Nagel? That is so adorable. You're rehired. Sucker! I knew exactly what she was talkin' about.
jokes.setup: Gary Coleman being fired and using his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Playing up the catchphrase for manipulation then revealing awareness
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of Gary Coleman's famous catchphrase showing strategic self-awareness

jokes.text: Do you have any idea how much pressure we're under to come up with a new Furby or Tickle Me Elmo? And thanks to you, Funzo is the first doll designed by children for children, with all the profits going to children. Really? Yeah. Well, we're all somebody's children.
jokes.setup: Executive explaining corporate pressure and claiming child-focused mission
jokes.punchline: Semantic dodge that makes adult executives technically children
jokes.explanation: Excellent corporate double-speak satire with clever linguistic manipulation

jokes.text: Thanks, Funzo. You rock. All righty. [Dance]
jokes.setup: Bart playing with his new Funzo doll
jokes.punchline: Funzo's enthusiastic dance response to praise
jokes.explanation: Standard toy interaction showing Funzo's appealing surface behavior.

jokes.text: Yeah. Funzo makes playtime fun! You mean like Microsoft? Exactly.
jokes.setup: Discussing Funzo eliminating toy competition
jokes.punchline: Casual comparison to Microsoft's monopolistic practices
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural commentary linking toy marketing to corporate monopoly tactics

jokes.text: I have a flower for 'ou.
jokes.setup: Lisa and Bart trying to warn people about dangerous Funzo
jokes.punchline: Funzo's cute gesture undermining their warning
jokes.explanation: Effective contrast between perceived threat and adorable behavior

jokes.text: Wow. Only 22 seconds from muttering to door smash. That projects to a profit of... $370 million.
jokes.setup: Crowd rushing to buy Funzo toys
jokes.punchline: Corporate calculation of mob violence into profit projections
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark satire of corporate profit calculation from consumer desperation

jokes.text: Now why would you wear cleats to a store?
jokes.setup: Person being trampled in toy store rush
jokes.punchline: Focusing on footwear choice rather than being trampled
jokes.explanation: Good misplaced priorities humor during chaotic situation

jokes.text: Now let's see. This'll make three Christmases I saved versus eight I ruined. Two were kind of a draw.
jokes.setup: Homer deciding to save Christmas by stealing toys
jokes.punchline: Homer's detailed accounting of his Christmas success rate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment showing his self-awareness and terrible track record

jokes.text: Writhing Funzos in my sack. Makes me happy. Makes me hurt my back.
jokes.setup: Homer collecting stolen Funzo dolls
jokes.punchline: Improvised song about toy theft causing back pain
jokes.explanation: Mild humor with Homer's improvised criminal song and physical comedy

jokes.text: I'm very mad at 'ou. [Groans]
jokes.setup: Funzo dolls being burned in fire
jokes.punchline: Funzo expressing anger in cute voice while being destroyed
jokes.explanation: Contrast between cute toy voice and dire situation. Mildly threatening.

jokes.text: Looks like the biggest rip-off since Webster.
jokes.setup: Gary Coleman discovering the toy company scheme
jokes.punchline: Coleman referencing his own TV show as a rip-off
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deprecating meta-joke with Coleman criticizing his own career

jokes.text: Well, isn't it possible for an evil company to make people happy? Are you saying the end justifies the means? That's a very glib interpretation.
jokes.setup: Discussion about corporate ethics
jokes.punchline: Former child star engaging in sophisticated philosophical debate
jokes.explanation: Good subversion showing Gary Coleman as unexpectedly philosophical

jokes.text: And so, Gary Coleman and the Simpsons argued long into the night. And then, as day broke, the spirit of the season entered their hearts.
jokes.setup: Extended philosophical argument between characters
jokes.punchline: Narrator summarizing unshown debate and convenient resolution
jokes.explanation: Clever narrative shortcut making fun of convenient Christmas story resolutions

jokes.text: Let's just agree that the commercialization of Christmas is, at best, a mixed blessing. Amen.
jokes.setup: Resolution of complex Christmas commercialization debate
jokes.punchline: Extremely mild, diplomatic conclusion to heated argument
jokes.explanation: Anticlimatic diplomatic resolution after promising intense philosophical debate.

jokes.text: Um, uh, Mr. Coleman, I've been thinking. Uh, my wife always makes too much stuffing and sweet 'potaters' and all. And- Oh, heck. Would you like to spend Christmas with us? No way. I'm having Christmas at George Clooney's house. All right. I'll come.
jokes.setup: Homer awkwardly inviting Gary Coleman for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Coleman's casual celebrity name-drop followed by immediate acceptance
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment with Coleman's celebrity claims and Homer's genuine invitation

jokes.text: As for old Mr. Burns, he was visited by three ghosts during the night and agreed to fund the school with some money he found in his tuxedo pants.
jokes.setup: Christmas Carol parody resolution for Burns
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of enormous wealth literally in his pockets
jokes.explanation: Standard Christmas resolution with Burns' casual extreme wealth.

jokes.text: Uh, listen. I kind of banged up that Jeep in the driveway. What you talkin' about, Moe? What you talkin' 'bout, everyone?
jokes.setup: Moe confessing to property damage during Christmas dinner
jokes.punchline: Gary Coleman's catchphrase spreading to everyone at the table
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that spreads Coleman's catchphrase to the whole family

tmdb_overview: In this Christmas-themed episode, the students of Springfield Elementary are unwittingly used as a focus group to test new toys. The product of their labors, a cuddly doll named Funzo, is a lot more sinister than he appears.
